---
format: html
---

# Measure your system, then collect your structured data (session four)

:::{.callout-important collapse=true appearance='default' icon=true}
## Outline blob

### Sentence aim
This session = doing the measurements once you know how to data-fy your system
### Exercises

### Data

### Key concepts

:::

Three weeks ago, we talked about some example datasets:

+ a month-by-month count of patients visiting our hospital for the last two years
+ census data, showing demographics of different towns in Scotland
+ survey data estimating how many people are currently homeless nationally

This session talks about how those datasets might be built, concentrating on the technical aspects of collecting and structuring data.

Roughly, to explain what your data should look like, and how to begin collecting useful data from your system. Those aspects can be very complex, but needn't be - so we could potentially collect data for our three example questions by:

* adding up all the inpatient and outpatient appointments each month
* asking everyone in Scotland to complete a census return every few years
* requesting services supporting the homeless to estimate how many service users they see each week

Builds on the last session about refining a question

## missing data
